description_long: This is a small, rustic barn with a classic gable roof covered in weathered wooden shingles. The upper portion of the structure features vertical wooden planks, while the lower half is clad in horizontal, aged wooden boards, giving it a two-tone appearance. The front side has a small, rectangular door set into the upper section, suggesting a loft or storage area, while the rear side has a larger, plain door at ground level. There is a single, small window on the back wall, providing minimal natural light. The barn's compact size and simple construction indicate it was likely used for storage or as a utility outbuilding. Its overall appearance is utilitarian, with visible signs of age and wear, fitting well into rural or peri-urban environments.
description: A small, weathered barn with a gable roof, vertical and horizontal wood siding, and minimal doors and windows. Its rustic, utilitarian design features a two-tone exterior and a shingled roof.
description_view_0: Front view shows the gable roof, vertical upper siding, and a small loft door centered near the roof peak.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the shingled roof, horizontal lower siding, and a large, plain door at ground level.
description_view_2: Opposite gable end with vertical upper siding and no visible doors or windows, mirroring the front but without the loft door.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the shingled roof, horizontal siding, and a small, single window near the center of the wall.
attribute_siding_pattern: vertical board and batten on upper section, horizontal lap siding on lower section
attribute_door_style: one small hinged loft door on the front, one large single hinged door on the side
attribute_window_arrangement: one small window centered on the rear wall, no other windows visible
